revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:藤切神社]]


{{Unreferenced|date=July 16, 2016}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|name   = Fujikiri Shrine
|image  =File:Fujikiri-shrine.jpg 
|location = {{ill|Higashiōmi|en|Higashiōmi|qid=Q240891|lt=Higashiōmi|1=Higashiōmi}}, Kōtsuhata-chō, Shiga Prefecture
|coordinates = {{Coord|35.048|N|136.321528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity = * {{ill|Three Munakata Go11esses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Go11esses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}
** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
** {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}
** {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}
|Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}
|Foundation = Traditionally dated to {{ill|Ten'ō (781)|ja|天応 (日本)|1=Ten'ō|2=ja|3=天応 (日本)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1373125}}{{citation needed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'ō (781)|qq=}} 1 (781 CE)
|Main Building = Three-bay {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|qid=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}
|Other names = 
|Festival = April 5
|Rituals = Fire Prevention Festival (January 8)
|location = {{ill|Higashiomi|qid=Q240891|1=Higashiōmi}}
}}
'''Fujikiri Shrine''' (藤切神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Higashiōmi|en|Higashiōmi|qid=Q240891|lt=Higashiōmi|1=Higashiōmi}}, Kōtsuhata-chō, Shiga Prefecture.

== Deities ==
* {{ill|Three Munakata Go11esses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Go11esses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}
** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
** {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}
** {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}

== Shrine Crest ==
* Left Mitsudomoe (三ッ巴)

== History ==
According to tradition, Fujikiri Shrine was founded in {{ill|Ten'ō (781)|ja|天応 (日本)|1=Ten'ō|2=ja|3=天応 (日本)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1373125}}{{citation needed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'ō (781)|qq=}} 1 (781 CE) by {{ill|Nakatomi no Kiyomaro|ja|中臣清麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kiyomaro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大中臣清麻呂|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11431854}}, who transferred the deities to this location. In ancient times, a major road connecting {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}} and {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|qid=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}} passed through this area, with the nearby {{ill|Chigusa Pass|ja|千草峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigusa Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} being a crucial crossroads. This pass later became famous as the site where {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}} was ambushed by {{ill|Sugitani Zenjūbō|en|Sugitani Zenjūbō|qid=Q11095190|lt=Sugitani Zenjūbō|1=Sugitani Zenjūbō}}.

The shrine received significant patronage from the {{ill|Sasaki clan|en|Sasaki clan|qid=Q1028587|lt=Sasaki clan|1=Sasaki clan}}, who contributed to the development of the shrine's buildings. The current shrine structure was rebuilt in {{ill|Kyōwa|en|Kyōwa|qid=Q960763|lt=Kyōwa|1=Kyōwa}} 2 (1802 CE). In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated as a village shrine in 1876.

== Festivals ==
* Fire Prevention Festival (January 8)
* Main Festival (April 5)

== Sub-Shrines ==
* {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}}
